reasoning: Keir Starmer won a historically large parliamentary majority in July 2024. A PM with such a commanding position is extremely unlikely to be removed by his party or Parliament in his first full calendar year. While his approval ratings have dipped due to minor scandals (e.g., donations) and policy backlash (winter fuel payments), an internal coup would be structurally and politically difficult this early. The main risks are unforeseen health issues or a catastrophic unprecedented scandal. Given historical base rates for newly elected PMs, the chance of him leaving office in 2025 is around 5-8%, somewhat below the market's 9%.
